evidence.title: Brief Summary
evidence.snippet:
A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel, exploratory phase 2a study to evaluate safety and biologic efficacy on wound healing of ILP100-Topical in subjects with diabetic foot ulcers during 26 weeks with a 5-year long-term follow-up period. A total of 30 subjects will be randomized to low dose of ILP100-Topical (ILP100Lo), high dose of ILP100-Topical (ILP100Hi) or Placebo according to a 1:1:1 randomization schedule.

The study will consist of a 3-weeks Screening and Run-in Phase,

opinions.reasoning: The trial description explicitly states "Topical application" of the biological agent.
The trial explicitly states that the ILP100-Topical is applied topically to the wound area, which fits the definition of Topical delivery.

The use of terms like "topical application" and "wound area" in the metadata strongly suggests that the treatment is being administered directly to the skin or mucosal surface, rather than through injection or oral ingestion. This aligns with the definition of a topical delivery mode.
The placebo arm includes "the activation peptide SppIP," which is a peptide. Since any peptide in an intervention makes the trial True, this qualifies.
The presence of "activation peptide SppIP" in the placebo formulation directly indicates that a peptide is part of the intervention, satisfying the definition of a peptide-containing intervention.

The description of ILP100-Topical as a "live biopharmaceutical product" and a "lactic acid bacteria" suggests that it is a type of probiotic or live biotherapeutic product, rather than a peptide molecule. Additionally, the presence of an activation peptide in the placebo arm does not necessarily indicate that the intervention itself contains a peptide molecule.

fields.reconciler_reasoning: The evidence explicitly mentions the presence of "activation peptide SppIP" in the placebo formulation. Since the trial involves a peptide (SppIP) as part of the intervention, even in the placebo arm, the field "peptide" should be marked as "True." The presence of a peptide in the intervention, regardless of whether it is in the active or placebo arm, satisfies the definition of a peptide-containing trial. The verifiers who agreed on "True" provided consistent and evidence-based reasoning, and t

opinions.reasoning: The clinical trial registry explicitly states that the trial was terminated due to patient recruitment issues.
The trial was terminated due to patient recruitment issues, as explicitly stated in the clinicaltrials.gov record.

The trial was terminated due to patient recruitment issues, which is a clear indication that the study failed to meet its enrollment goals. This reason for termination is directly stated on ClinicalTrials.gov and provides a specific explanation for why the trial was halted. There is no published evidence of negative outcomes or safety concerns, so I have assigned a high confidence level to this answer.

fields.reconciler_reasoning: The clinicaltrials.gov record explicitly states that the trial was terminated due to "patient recruitment issues," which directly indicates that the trial failed to meet its enrollment goals. This is a clear and specific reason for failure, and it is supported by multiple verifiers who have independently cited this evidence. The primary annotator's answer of "Toxic/Unsafe" is not supported by any evidence in the provided data, and therefore the correct answer is "Recruitment issues."

identificationModule.briefTitle: Evaluating Safety and Biological Effect on Wound Healing of ILP100-Topical in Subjects With Diabetic Foot Ulcers
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-controlled, Parallel, Exploratory Phase 2a Study to Evaluate Safety and Biological Effect on Wound Healing of ILP100-Topical in Subjects With Diabetic Foot Ulcers

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel, exploratory phase 2a study to evaluate safety and biologic efficacy on wound healing of ILP100-Topical in subjects with diabetic foot ulcers during 26 weeks with a 5-year long-term follow-up period. A total of 30 subjects will be randomized to low dose of ILP100-Topical (ILP100Lo), high dose of ILP100-Topical (ILP100Hi) or Placebo according to a 1:1:1 randomization schedule.

The study will consist of a 3-weeks Screening and Run-in Phase, followed by a 5-week Treatment Phase starting from Baseline and an Assessment Phase from Week 5 to Week 26. Thereafter, the subjects will be followed yearly during 5 years in a Long-Term Safety Follow-up Phase.

maskingInfo.maskingDescription: This is a double-blind study, and the allocation of treatments will not be disclosed to subjects, Investigator or Independent Evaluators until database lock after all subjects (who were not withdrawn) completed Week 26.

armGroups.label: ILP100Lo
armGroups.description: During the Treatment Phase, subjects will continue on standard of care according to the protocol and ILP100Lo (ILP100-Topical, 5x10\^7 colony forming units (CFU)/cm\^2) will be topically administered at two occasions on Day 1 (Baseline and Hour 2), Days 2, 3, and thereafter every second to third day until Day 31.

armGroups.label: ILP100Hi
armGroups.description: During the Treatment Phase, subjects will continue on standard of care according to the protocol and ILP100Hi (ILP100-Topical, 1x10\^9 CFU/cm\^2) will be topically administered at two occasions on Day 1 (Baseline and Hour 2), Days 2, 3, and thereafter every second to third day until Day 31.

armGroups.label: Placebo
armGroups.description: During the Treatment Phase, subjects will continue on standard of care according to the protocol and Placebo (ILP100 dilution buffer mixed with the activation peptide SppIP) will be topically administered at two occasions on Day 1 (Baseline and Hour 2), Days 2, 3, and thereafter every second to third day until Day 31.

interventions.name: ILP100-Topical (emilimogene sigulactibac) 5x10^7 CFU/cm^2
interventions.description: Topical application of ILP100-Topical (emilimogene sigulactibac) at a dose of 5x10\^7 CFU/cm\^2 wound area.

interventions.name: ILP100-Topical (emilimogene sigulactibac) 1x10^9 CFU/cm^2
interventions.description: Topical application of ILP100-Topical (emilimogene sigulactibac) at a dose of 1x10\^9 CFU/cm\^2 wound area.

interventions.name: Placebo
interventions.description: Topical application of placebo (ILP100 dilution buffer mixed with the activation peptide SppIP).

primaryOutcomes.measure: Compare incidence of adverse events (AEs) between treatment groups
primaryOutcomes.description: The incidence of AEs up to Week 26 will be presented for each treatment group and by category, causality, severity and frequency.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Compare incidence of serious adverse events (SAEs) between treatment groups
primaryOutcomes.description: The incidence of SAEs up to Week 26 will be presented for each treatment group and by category, causality, severity and frequency.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

secondaryOutcomes.description: Local tolerance to treatment will be assessed by the Investigator. Wound tolerability parameters will be presented in categories of appearance of inflammation, amount of exudate present, hemorrhage, presence of slough/necrotic tissue, presence of granulation tissue and hypergranulation.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare Investigator's local tolerability assessments up to Week 26 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Local tolerability by Independent Assessor's assessment
secondaryOutcomes.description: Local tolerance to treatment will be assessed by the Independent Assessor. Wound tolerability parameters will be presented in categories of appearance of inflammation, amount of exudate present, hemorrhage, presence of slough/necrotic tissue, presence of granulation tissue and hypergranulation.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare local tolerability up to Week 26 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Proportion of subjects with a local tolerability parameter Grade ≥ 2 per Investigator's Assessment
secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of subjects with a local tolerability parameter grade ≥ 2 will be calculated (grading system=0 to 3, where 3 is severe). Local tolerance to treatment, infection and wound healing will be assessed by the Investigator. Wound tolerability parameters include appearance of inflammation, amount of exudate present, hemorrhage, presence of slough/necrotic tissue, presence of granulation tissue and hypergranulation.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Proportion of subjects with a local tolerability parameter Grade ≥ 2 per Independent Assessor's Assessment
secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of subjects with a local tolerability parameter grade ≥ 2 will be calculated (grading system=0 to 3, where 3 is severe). Local tolerance to treatment, infection and wound healing will be assessed by the Independent Assessor. Wound tolerability parameters include appearance of inflammation, amount of exudate present, hemorrhage, presence of slough/necrotic tissue, presence of granulation tissue and hypergranulation.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

secondaryOutcomes.description: Wound area will be measured and calculated as proportion of the wound area at Baseline. Metric analyses of wound area is made on 3D models acquired up to Week 26.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.description: Wound healing rate will be calculated as change in the wound area per week. Wound healing will be assessed by Investigators and Independent Assessor.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Proportion of subjects with ≥50% and 75% partially healed wounds
secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of subjects with ≥50% and 75% partially healed wounds will be calculated.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Time to partial (≥50% and 75%) and complete healing
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to event outcomes will be evaluated using Kaplan-Meier curves. Wound healing is defined as full skin re-epithelialization without drainage or dressing requirements confirmed at two consecutive study visits 2 weeks apart. Wound healing will be assessed by Investigators and Independent Assessor.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.description: Wound area will be measured and calculated (cm\^2). Metric analyses of wound healing, including area, are made using 3D models acquired Screening to Week 26. Wound measures will be automatically generated by a computer software and validated by the Independent Assessor.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Proportion of subjects with completely healed wounds
secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of subjects with completely healed wounds will be calculated. Wound healing is defined as full skin re-epithelialization without drainage or dressing requirements confirmed at two consecutive study visits 2 weeks apart. Wound healing will be assessed by Investigators and the Independent Assessor.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Proportion of subjects with recurrence of index wound
secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of subjects with recurrence of index wound will be calculated. For wound assessments after each index wound healing visit, it will be documented if the index wound is still healed or if any recurrence of the wound has occurred.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Proportion of subjects with new wound infections
secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of subjects with new wound infections will be calculated based on the Investigators evaluation of the skin, including assessing whether there are any new wound infections, at each study visit.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.description: Days on antibiotic treatment as a percentage of days in the study up to Week 26 will be calculated for each subject.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in wound-related pain in the area of the index wound, as reported by subjects, using a numeric rating scale (NRS; 0-10, where 10 is the worst possible pain).
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26

secondaryOutcomes.description: AEs and SAEs will be recorded during annual visits from Year 1 through Year 5.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Year 5 after initiation of treatment.

secondaryOutcomes.description: CXCL12 levels in plasma as measured by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA).
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare up to Week 16 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Presence of viable L. reuteri containing the pSIP_CXCL12 plasmid in wounds
secondaryOutcomes.description: Bacteria will be collected from the wound and perilesional skin with sterile cotton swabs which will be cultured.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare up to Year 5 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: Proportion of subjects with index limb amputation and any limb amputation
otherOutcomes.description: The proportion of subjects with index limb amputation and any limb amputation will be calculated.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare at Week 26, and thereafter annually Year 1 to 5, between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: All-cause mortality and index wound-related mortality
otherOutcomes.description: Mortality data will be acquired from the patient's medical records.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare at Week 26, and thereafter annually Year 1 to 5, between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: Quality of life assessed with Wound-Quality of Life (Wound QoL) questionnaire
otherOutcomes.description: Quality of life will be assessed by the subjects using the Wound-QoL questionnaire including 17 questions on impairment related to the wound, each to be answered by grades from 0-4 (0='not at all' to 4='very much')
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare up to Week 16 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.description: Time needed for IMP preparation, by the study personnel, will be recorded.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare at Week 4 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: Total number of redressings, off-loading and devices used for the treatment of the index wound
otherOutcomes.description: Wound management will include redressing, which will be done both during home visits and study site visits, while debridement and changes of off-loading devices should only be done at study site visits. The total number of redressings, off-loading and devices used for the treatment of the index wound will be recorded.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms during 16 weeks from baseline.

otherOutcomes.description: Metric analyses of wound healing, including volume, are made using 3D models acquired from Screening to Week 26. Wound measures will be automatically generated by a computer software and validated by the Independent Assessor.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.description: Metric analyses of wound healing, including depth, are made using 3D models acquired from Screening to Week 26. Wound measures will be automatically generated by a computer software and validated by the Independent Assessor.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.description: Reduction of wound volume (percent of Baseline) will be calculated. Metric analyses of wound healing, including volume, are made using 3D models acquired from Screening to Week 26. Wound measures will be automatically generated by a computer software and validated by the Independent Assessor.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.description: Reduction of wound depth (percent of Baseline) will be calculated. Metric analyses of wound healing, including depth, are made using 3D models acquired from Screening to Week 26. Wound measures will be automatically generated by a computer software and validated by the Independent Assessor.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.measure: Analyses of predictive factors for wound healing outcome
otherOutcomes.description: Analyses of associations between baseline clinical (including toe pressure at Baseline and sex) and biologic characteristics (including wound size and pH) with wound healing outcomes through Week 26. A statistical regression model will be developed to predict wound healing up to Week 26
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Analyses of associations between baseline clinical and biologic characteristics and wound healing outcomes up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.measure: Analyses of predictive factors for occurence of AEs
otherOutcomes.description: Analyses of associations between baseline clinical (including toe pressure at Baseline and sex) and biologic characteristics (including wound size and pH) with AEs. A statistical regression model will be developed to predict the AE outcome up to Week 26
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Analyses of associations between baseline clinical and biologic characteristics and occurence of AEs up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.description: Scar area will be measured for the index wound using 3D models generated from superimposing 2D images and infrared scanning of the topography of the wound and perilesional skin acquired at the study visits.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.description: Scar volume will be measured for the index wound using 3D models generated from superimposing 2D images and infrared scanning of the topography of the wound and perilesional skin acquired at the study visits.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 26.

otherOutcomes.measure: Change from Baseline in level of wound pH
otherOutcomes.description: Change from Baseline in level of wound pH will be measured on exuding wounds (at the discretion of the Investigator) at selected timepoints just prior to IMP administration using sterile litmus paper strips.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare at Weeks 1, 4, 5, 8 and 16 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: Change from Baseline in local blood flow in wound area measured with Laser Speckle Contrast Analysis
otherOutcomes.description: Change from Baseline in local blood flow around the index wound will be addressed by non-invasive imaging using Laser Speckle Contrast Analysis (LASCA) where each wound and surrounding skin will be recorded for at least one minute of stable recording at selected timepoints in the study.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms up to Week 16

otherOutcomes.measure: Pathogenic wound bacteria characterization upon clinically defined or suspicions of wound infection
otherOutcomes.description: Characteristics of ILP100 will be addressed by collecting bacteria from the wound with sterile cotton swabs which will be cultured.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare species of infection-causing wound pathogens up to Week 26 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: Wound microbiome profile, including presence and proportion of wound pathogenic bacteria
otherOutcomes.description: The impact of ILP100 on the wound microbiome composition will be assessed from wounds swabs identified by sequencing bacterial 16S ribosomal ribonucleic acid (rRNA) genes.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare the microbiome profile up to Week 16 between the ILP100 and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: Presence of anti-drug antibodies (ADA) to CXCL12 and the activation peptide SppIP up to Year 5
otherOutcomes.description: The development of anti-drug antibodies (ADAs, to CXCL12 and SppIP) as measured by electrochemiluminescence immunoassay (ECLIA).
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Compare presence of ADA up to Year 5 between the ILP100-Topical and Placebo treatment arms.

otherOutcomes.measure: Analysis of L. reuteri containing the pSIP_CXCL12 plasmid in blood, feces, and perilesional skin
otherOutcomes.description: Shedding to the external environment will be measured in feces and water lock samples collected at the sites. The method will determine the number of live L. reuteri R2LC containing pSIP\_CXCL12 in the samples. Used dressings will also be collected for shedding analyses of L. reuteri R2LC containing the pSIP\_CXCL12 plasmid.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Analyses of presence of L. reuteri containing the pSIP_CXCL12 plasmid up to Year 5.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Signed written informed consent
* Males and females aged ≥18 years
* Diagnosis of diabetes mellitus type 1 or 2
* HbA1c ≤ 86 mmol/mol (≤ 10%) at Screening
* Subjects with at least one first time or recurrent full thickness ulcer (at or below the ankle) which fulfils all of the following criteria at Screening and at the time of Baseline:

  1. A non-interdigital wound
  2. Accessible for administration of IMP, wound study assessments and procedures
  3. Persistence of the wound for at least 6 weeks at Baseline
  4. Assessed by the investigator to be of non-venous etiology.
  5. Minimum full skin ulcer without undermining, with no exposed muscle, tendon or bone
  6. A wound area of 1.0 - 5.0 cm\^2 after sharp or mechanical debridement at Screening
  7. During the 2-weeks between start of Run-in Phase and Baseline the wound size must not decrease by more than 30% or increase by more than 25%, which correspond to wound areas of 0.7 - 6.3 cm\^2, or at Screening expected by the Investigator to have a high probability for wound size changes within this range during this period.
* Toe pressure ≥20 mm Hg
* Expected to comply with the study procedures

Exclusion Criteria:

* Infected index wound with clinical signs of inflammation at Screening or Baseline.
* The index wound determined as heavily exuding defined as requiring more than 1 dressing change per day or requiring use of super absorbent dressing
* Wound duration longer than 2 years
* Active Charcot deformity of the study foot
* Estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) \<30 mL/min/1.73m\^2
* Hemoglobin concentration \<100 g/L at Baseline
* Planned or ongoing treatment with corticosteroids to an equivalent dose of prednisolone \>10 mg per day or other immunosuppressive therapy, or such treatment within 4 weeks prior to Baseline
* Has any major surgery or hospitalization planned up to Week 26
* Has changed a treatment for diabetes during the last 3 weeks before Baseline. Dose change is allowed
* Ongoing treatment with dipeptidyl peptidase 4 (DPP-4) inhibitors
* Revascularization procedure in the index wound leg planned or undertaken within 8 weeks before Screening, or under investigation
* Current smokers
* Participation in other clinical studies or having received any investigational treatment within 1 month or at the earliest five times the half-life prior to Screening
* Has any disease conditions, including ulcerative dermatological disorders and vasculitis, or comorbidities which is expected to prevent the subject from participating in the study or confounding the evaluation of the safety profile and effect on wound healing of ILP100
* Pregnant or lactating woman
* Male subjects not willing to use a condom and refrain from donating sperm
* Female subjects of childbearing potential unless they use a contraceptive method with a failure rate of \< 1% to prevent pregnancy
